text.title: Experiment Verdict Template Worked Example
text.summary: Worked example: pricing-page hero variant with primary win + secondary tension.

section.title: Scenario
section.p: EXP-2026-Q2-031: B-short vs control for /pricing hero. Pre-reg sample 10k, close 2026-05-20. n=12.4k reached on schedule. Primary metric: CTA CTR. Secondary: signup CR, D7 retention.

section.title: Verdict (markdown)
section.code:
## EXP-2026-Q2-031 — Pricing-page hero variant B
- **hypothesis:** Shortening the hero copy increases primary CTA clicks for cold pricing traffic. - **arms:** control (long copy) / B (short copy) - **primary:** CTA CTR — B +6.2% (95% CI [+2.1%, +10.3%], p=0.004) - **secondary:** - signup conversion — B -1.8% (CI [-4.0%, +0.4%], NS) - 7-day retention — flat - **verdict:** ship-treatment with monitoring (CTA win clean, signup non-significant within CI) - **exec_sign_off:** null (secondary regression NS, no override needed) - **learning:** - claim: Shorter hero copy on cold pricing traffic improves CTA CTR with no clear bottom-of-funnel regression. - confidence: moderate - **routed:** - owner: @marina - target_date: 2026-05-23 - ticket: REL-1402 - **closed_at:** 2026-05-20T17:00:00Z - **monitoring:** alert on signup_cr if 14-day window shows < -3% vs control.

list.item: Closed within 5 business days of close_date (rule close-within-5-days).
Secondary regressions addressed; non-significant so no exec_sign_off required (rule secondary-metric-gate).
Learning captured with confidence tag (rule captured-learning).
Routed to named owner with ticket + date (rule routed-action).
